description_long: This is a small, elevated wooden house with vertical striped paneling in natural wood tones. The structure stands on stilts, raising it above the ground, and is accessed by a central staircase leading to a covered porch with a simple wooden railing. The roof is a large, corrugated metal gable, providing ample coverage and protection from the elements. The house features several rectangular windows with wooden shutters, some of which are open, and the entryway is set back under the porch roof. The overall design is utilitarian, with an emphasis on ventilation and protection from flooding, typical of tropical or rural stilt houses.
description: A small, stilted wooden house with a corrugated metal gable roof, covered porch, and shuttered windows in a striped wood pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central staircase, covered porch, and main entryway with two shuttered windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended gable roof, two open windows, and the depth of the porch area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall with a single window and the full width of the corrugated roof.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the side and front, with the staircase, porch, and multiple open windows visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Central staircase leading to a covered porch with wooden railing and a recessed main door
